title: Tommy Martyn

[[Shay Martyn]] (son) }} }} '''Thomas Martyn''' (born 4 June 1971) is a former [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland international]] [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1980s, 1990s and 2000s, and [[Coach (sport)|coach]]ed in the 2000s. He played at representative level for [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]], and at club level for [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] and [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] in the [[Rugby Football League Championship|Championship]], and, subsequently, [[Super League]] and [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]], as a {{rlp|so}}. Martyn was known for his very good passing ability and vision. He was able to enjoy a successful rugby career despite undergoing two knee reconstructions.{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/3009512.stm|publisher=BBC News|title=Martyn quits Saints for Leigh|date=2003-06-21|access-date=25 May 2010}} ==Background== Martyn was born in [[Leigh, Greater Manchester|Leigh]], [[Lancashire]], England. ==Playing career== The son of the rugby league footballer [[Thomas Martyn (rugby league)|Tommy Martyn Sr]], and the nephew of the Leigh and Great Britain rugby league footballer [[Mick Martyn (rugby league)|Mick Martyn]],{{cite web|url=http://www.thisislancashire.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/leighcenturions/4532358.Leigh_RLFC_Past_Players_Association_dinner|title=Leigh RLFC Past Players Association dinner|publisher=thisislancashire.co.uk|date=6 August 2009|access-date=1 January 2012}} Martyn played for St Helens from the interchange bench in their [[1996 Challenge Cup]] Final victory over [[Bradford Bulls]].{{cite web|title=steveprescottfoundation.co.uk|url=http://www.steveprescottfoundation.co.uk/About/StevePrescottStats.aspx|work=Steve Prescott Stats|publisher=Steve Prescott Foundation|access-date=3 June 2012}} Tommy Martyn Jr. teamed up with [[Bobbie Goulding]] in the halves for the 1997 [[Challenge Cup]] Final at Wembley. His two tries and try-saving [[ankle tap]] tackle on [[Danny Peacock]] helped St. Helens to victory and earned him the [[Lance Todd Trophy]] as man-of-the-match. {{cite news |last = Kelner |first = Simon |title = Saints go shining through the hype |work = The Independent |date = 1997-05-04 |url = https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-saints-go-shining-through-the-hype-1259756.html |access-date = 5 December 2009 |location=London}} Martyn played for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] at scrum half back in their [[1999 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[Bradford Bulls]]. Having won the [[1999 Super League Grand Final]], [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] contested in the [[2000 World Club Challenge]] against [[National Rugby League]] Premiers [[Melbourne Storm|Melbourne]], with Martyn playing as a stand-off half back in the loss. He also played for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] at stand-off half back in their [[2000 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]]. In 2000, Martyn's abilities were rewarded when he was selected to represent [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] in the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup]] where he represented the Wolfhounds at Stand-off defeating [[Samoa]] and Scotland in the pool stages and losing to England 26–16 in the quarter finals where he scored a try and kicked 2 goals. As [[Super League V]] champions, [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] played against [[2000 NRL season|2000 NRL]] Premiers, [[Brisbane Broncos|Brisbane]] in the [[2001 World Club Challenge]]. Martyn played at stand-off half back in St. Helens' victory. Having won [[Super League VI]], St Helens contested the [[2003 World Club Challenge]] against 2002 NRL Premiers, [[Sydney Roosters]]. Martyn played at scrum half back in St. Helens' 38–0 loss. ==Post playing== In 2008 he joined the coaching staff of Super League side [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]], to act as a kicking coach for the academy side.{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12196_3407155,00.html|title=Martyn to add Wolves bite|publisher=Sky Sports|date=2008-04-10|access-date=10 April 2008}} In December 2009 he joined [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] in a similar role.{{cite web|url=http://www.runcornandwidnesworld.co.uk/vikings/vikingsnews/4789370.Martyn_kicks_in|title=Martyn kicks in|publisher=Runcorn and Widnes World|date=2009-12-11|access-date=3 August 2012}}{{dead link|date=July 2018|bot=medic}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}} Martyn's son [[Shay Martyn]] is a professional rugby league footballer who plays for St Helens in the [[Super League]]. ==References== {{reflist}} {{S-start}} {{S-sport}} {{succession box | before = [[Darren Abram]]
